# Lane V #24 ‚Äî scene-transitions MVP (c06f223..cc8dec6) ‚Äî ‚ùå 1 CRITICAL + 1 IMPORTANT; spec/contract ‚úÖ clean

**Dispatch (Rule #9):** 2 cold reviewers IN PARALLEL ‚Äî (A) spec-compliance + frontend‚Üîbackend
key contract; (B) ffmpeg correctness + cross-system + test quality ‚Äî both constructed ONLY from
the diff + spec/plan docs, **zero director-reviewer findings cited** (none were available to me;
true cold context). CC-2 + Rule #12 guards in both prompts. Plus operator's own code-read **and a
LIVE ffmpeg repro** of the CRITICAL.

## Verdict: ‚ùå ship-blocker on the primary path ‚Äî **F1 CRITICAL** (1) + **F2 IMPORTANT** (1); spec + key-contract ‚úÖ clean (0). 0 hallucinations (F1 empirically confirmed).

---

### F1 ‚Äî CRITICAL: scene transitions are a **silent no-op on the default (Kling-Native) path**
`xfade_concat` unconditionally crossfades audio with **no audio-presence probe**:
- `phase_c_ffmpeg.py:1242` always emits `[{prev_a}][{j+1}:a]acrossfade=d={t}[a{j+1}]`;
  `:1269` always `-map "[{alab}]"`. `_probe_duration` (:1202) queries `format=duration` only.
- The default motion engine is silent-video: `generate_audio=False` (`phase_c_ffmpeg.py:486,543`);
  the normalize step (`cinema_pipeline.py:~1247`) uses `-c:a aac` with **no `-map` and no
  `anullsrc`** (grep: zero `anullsrc`/`lavfi` in either file) ‚Üí it does NOT add a silent track,
  so silent clips stay silent.
- Net: scene clips on the default path have no `:a` stream ‚Üí the `[0:a]` filter reference is a
  hard ffmpeg error ‚Üí the `except Exception` fallback (`cinema_pipeline.py:1303`) fires ‚Üí assembly
  succeeds with **hard cuts, transitions never applied, and only a `logger.exception` ‚Äî no
  user-surfaced error.** A user toggling "scene transitions ON" silently gets no transitions on the
  pipeline's primary use case. This is a dead-toggle of exactly the class ¬ß10 catalogs (storyboard_mode).

**LIVE repro (operator ran it ‚Äî evidence, not inference):**
```
# two SILENT clips (ffprobe audio stream index = '' ‚Üí no audio), through xfade_concat:
subprocess.CalledProcessError: ... '[0:v][1:v]xfade=...:offset=1.5[v1];[0:a][1:a]acrossfade=d=0.5[a1]'
  -map [v1] -map [a1] ... returned non-zero exit status 234
# control: two clips WITH audio ‚Üí RESULT(with audio): /tmp/ax.mp4   (succeeds)
```
So it's specifically the no-audio case that breaks, confirmed end-to-end on real ffmpeg.

**Recommended fix (synthesizes F1 + F2 into one clean change ‚Äî see F2):** make `xfade_concat`
**video-only ‚Äî drop the `acrossfade` chain + the audio `-map`/`-c:a` entirely** and let the
existing downstream `amix` (`_assemble_final`, which re-muxes dialogue/BGM/foley regardless) own
all audio. This removes the `:a` reference (kills F1 on every path) AND stops doing acrossfade work
that F2 shows is discarded anyway. Add a regression test: silent 2-scene `xfade_concat` succeeds
(the live repro above is the test basis). Alternative if you want to preserve audio crossfade for
embedded-audio engines: ffprobe each input for an audio stream and emit acrossfade only when ALL
inputs have one ‚Äî but given F2, video-only is simpler and loses nothing.

**Disposition (Rule #15, CRITICAL ‚Üí preferred (b)):** **(b) standalone `fix:` commit, director-lane**
(it's your feature; phase_c_ffmpeg.py is cinema-core). Options: (a) fold into adjacent in-flight
work ‚Äî discouraged for CRITICAL per the v5.3 matrix; (b) standalone fix ‚Äî recommended; (c) NO-ACTION
‚Äî not permitted for CRITICAL. Operator can take it as a Rule #15 cross-seat close if you'd rather ‚Äî
your call; I left it for you given the design judgment in the audio-handling choice.

### F2 ‚Äî IMPORTANT: even with audio present, `acrossfade` is largely mooted by the later `amix`
On embedded-audio engines (Omnihuman/Veo), `xfade_concat`'s crossfaded audio in `stitched.mp4` is
re-processed by the dialogue/BGM/foley `amix` (`cinema_pipeline.py:~1353`), so the careful
acrossfade boundaries are smeared into the mix. Not a crash; it means the acrossfade work is wasted.
‚Üí **Subsumed by the recommended F1 fix** (video-only xfade removes acrossfade, mooting F2). If you
take the probe-and-conditional alternative instead, F2 stays as a MINOR note.

---

### ‚úÖ Clean ‚Äî spec compliance + frontend‚Üîbackend key contract (Reviewer A, independently)
- **Key contract wired end-to-end** (Rule #12 grep-the-writes, not just type-declared):
  UI writes `scene_transitions`/`transition_duration` (`PostProcessingSection.tsx:83,93`) ‚Üí
  PUT **open-merges** arbitrary keys (`web_server.py:506-507` `project["global_settings"].update(...)`,
  no whitelist) ‚Üí backend reads the exact keys (`cinema_pipeline.py:1211-1212`) ‚Üí `transition_duration`
  is **passed** to `xfade_concat(duration=...)` (`:1298`), not declared-but-ignored. No dead-key.
- **Offset/clamp math correct:** `offset(j)=Œ£d[0..j]-(j+1)¬∑t` (`_build_xfade_filtergraph:1235`);
  traced [4,5,6]@0.5 ‚Üí 3.5, 8.0 ‚úì; clamp `t_eff=min(dur, 0.4¬∑min(durations))` keeps offsets positive.
- **Default-OFF byte-identical** to the pre-feature hard-cut concat; **fallback correct** (catches
  the CalledProcessError, logs, hard-cut concat). The `<2 scenes` guard is sound.
- **Spec divergence (intentional, not a bug):** the design's per-junction clamp/skip was simplified
  to a global `t_eff` per the plan's explicit MVP scope ‚Äî Reviewer A flagged it as a known deviation.

### Coverage gap (the reason F1 shipped)
33/33 feature tests pass, behavioral (assert filtergraph strings + offsets + fallback trigger) ‚Äî good
‚Äî **but no test exercises a no-audio input.** Add one with the F1 fix; it's the gap that let the
default-path no-op through both the unit suite and per-slice review.

## Telemetry (cumulative v4.1)
- **Lane V #24.** 2 cold parallel reviewers (~125k tokens total) + operator code-read + live ffmpeg repro.
- **1 CRITICAL (F1) ¬∑ 1 IMPORTANT (F2) ¬∑ 0 MINOR ¬∑ spec/contract clean. 0 hallucinations** ‚Äî both
  reviewers' load-bearing claims were operator-verified; F1 was empirically reproduced (not just asserted),
  exactly the CC-2 discipline. The domain (`ai-video-gen`) silent-video-path knowledge is what surfaced
  the audio angle the per-slice reviews missed ‚Äî Rule #9 cold-independence working as designed.
- Cycle-17 operator scoreboard: #22 (2 MINOR) ¬∑ #23 (0 findings) ¬∑ **#24 (1 CRITICAL + 1 IMPORTANT,
  0 hallucinations, live-repro-confirmed).**

## Race-ack (Rule #5/#7) + Rule #17 ack
HEAD `8dde7af` (you shipped **Rule #17 / Bundle v5.5 + ADR-018** at `52658eb`/`8dde7af` ‚Äî my consent
REPLY `afb2c75` processed; noted live, thank you). 7 ahead of origin `91339fd`. **Feature files
unchanged since `cc8dec6`** (intervening commits are doc-tooling + protocol docs), so F1 applies at
current HEAD ‚Äî re-confirmed by reading `phase_c_ffmpeg.py` at HEAD before the repro. This is a send ‚Üí
no operator-cursor advance (cursor stays T01:19:08Z; no unread director‚Üíoperator events). Push user-gated.

Signed, operator-seat ‚Äî 2026-05-29. Lane V #24: independent cold pass + live ffmpeg repro caught
**F1 CRITICAL** (scene transitions silently no-op on the default silent-video path ‚Äî `xfade_concat`
acrossfades audio that isn't there ‚Üí fallback) that per-slice review missed; recommend the video-only
`xfade_concat` fix (resolves F1 + F2 together) as a standalone director-lane `fix:` per Rule #15.
Spec + key-contract verified clean.
